title: Fix Karpenter's over-aggressive consolidation
scenario: 🔥 Karpenter is disrupting nodes every 10 minutes, causing pod evictions and 5xx spikes. The NodePool uses `WhenEmptyOrUnderutilized` with `consolidateAfter: 10m` — every minor traffic dip triggers node consolidation, evicting pods mid-request. Fix: switch to `WhenEmpty` consolidation and set `consolidateAfter: 30m` for production stability.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: karpenter.sh/v1
kind: NodePool
metadata:
  name: default
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      requirements:
      - key: karpenter.sh/capacity-type
        operator: In
        values: ["on-demand", "spot"]
      - key: node.kubernetes.io/instance-type
        operator: In
        values: ["m5.large", "m5.xlarge", "m5.2xlarge"]
  limits:
    cpu: "100"
    memory: 400Gi
  disruption:
    consolidationPolicy: WhenEmptyOrUnderutilized
    consolidateAfter: 10m

hints.text: `WhenEmptyOrUnderutilized` considers a node underutilized if its pods could fit on fewer nodes — which triggers at 10m intervals during normal traffic variation.
`WhenEmpty` only consolidates nodes that have zero pods. Much safer for production: pods are never evicted unless they've already moved away.

solution:
apiVersion: karpenter.sh/v1
kind: NodePool
metadata:
  name: default
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      requirements:
      - key: karpenter.sh/capacity-type
        operator: In
        values: ["on-demand", "spot"]
      - key: node.kubernetes.io/instance-type
        operator: In
        values: ["m5.large", "m5.xlarge", "m5.2xlarge"]
  limits:
    cpu: "100"
    memory: 400Gi
  disruption:
    consolidationPolicy: WhenEmpty
    consolidateAfter: 30m
